body:
APPROVE
Tracker-commit add/commit pair is wrapped in withTrackerLock at execute_bead.go:1037-1071, lockfile at .ddx/.git-tracker.lock, mkdir-based primitive parallels cli/internal/bead/lock.go with pid + acquired_at stale detection. Both required tests present; re-check inside lock and cached-diff bailout add nice robustness.

merged onto current tip

Both required tests present; re-check inside lock and cached-diff bailout add nice robustness.

post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)
Diff contains only a result.json execution-evidence file. No code changes for the tracker-commit lock, no lockfile, no tests. None of the 7 acceptance criteria are implemented in the reviewable diff.
result_rev=f97caec4e3206084ac5b19aa19f2b26d0bcb707d
base_rev=d9d1c1b8f9d74d3a4aa5da0cc8f04359fe8d33c6

Bead ddx-da11a34a is already fully implemented in the base revision
(8c0f4bdd) of this worktree. No new code change is required in this
attempt.

Implementation history (both commits are ancestors of HEAD):

- 3503f1f0 fix(agent): lock tracker-commit to prevent .git/index.lock race [ddx-da11a34a]
    Adds withTrackerLock + per-OS process-alive helpers and wraps the
    tracker-commit pair in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (now at
    lines ~1035-1071) so concurrent `ddx work --local` workers serialize
    git index operations on the primary .git.
- ba6882a4 fix(agent): expose tracker-lock retry/backoff policy [ddx-da11a34a]
    Adds LockRetryPolicy + DefaultLockRetryPolicy and the matching
    TestTrackerCommit_RetryBackoffPolicy suite.

Acceptance criteria mapping (verified against current worktree):

1. Tracker-commit pair wrapped in process-shared lock
   -> cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1039 withTrackerLock(...) wraps
      both `git add` and `git commit -m msg` calls.
2. Lockfile at .ddx/.git-tracker.lock
   -> cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:94 trackerLockPath returns
      filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ".git-tracker.lock").
3. Lock primitive reuses bead/lock_unix.go + lock_windows.go pattern
   -> cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_unix.go and tracker_lock_windows.go
      mirror the bead-store lock shape (Mkdir-based atomic lockdir,
      pid + acquired_at sidecar files, signal(0)/Windows process probe
      for staleness).
4. Lock-error retry policy owned here (not duplicated in triage)
   -> LockRetryPolicy struct + DefaultLockRetryPolicy + step() in
      tracker_lock.go:24-78. Docstring at tracker_lock.go:12-23 records
      the contract and points triage at ActionRetryWithBackoff.
5. Aligns with TD-031 §8.5 LockContentionContract; no persisted status
   change on contention; bd/br ADR-004 compatibility preserved.
   -> No status mutation on the contention path; only an error is
      returned, which one layer up maps to execution_failed via the
      existing populateWorkerStatus path. JSONL schema is untouched.
6. Tests present and green:
   -> TestTrackerCommit_ConcurrentSafety  (tracker_lock_test.go:54)
   -> TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery (tracker_lock_test.go:115)
   -> TestTrackerCommit_RetryBackoffPolicy (tracker_lock_test.go:160)
   `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run TestTrackerCommit -count=1`
   passes in this worktree (0.319s).
7. 5+ minute drain re-run is an out-of-band manual verification step,
   not a code change. The unit tests cover the contention shape; the
   drain-window verification is the operator's job.

Why the prior REVIEW:BLOCK note is now obsolete:

The note in the bead refers to an earlier attempt whose merged diff
contained only a result.json evidence file. Since then, two follow-up
attempts (3503f1f0 and ba6882a4) landed the actual code, tests, and
lock primitive on main, and they are reachable from the base of this
worktree. There is therefore nothing left to implement — re-doing the
work would only churn the file.

No code changes have been committed in this attempt.

No code changes have been committed in this attempt.
